description:
A versatile mind mapping tool for collaborative brainstorming.

EdrawMind is designed for creating fresh new visual innovations, integrating your bullet points to structure an overall mind map. It is convenient and helpful for you to use EdrawMind to sort out your thoughts or ideas in order to find solutions to problems.
The all-new EdrawMind AI workbench is now available
Enter a prompt to automatically generate a mind map, presentation, or project kanban. Try one of the “Inspiration” prompts on the Homepage. These are ready to use — no experience or learning necessary.
Supports one-click AI kanban generation
Describe your project goals in one prompt. The AI assistant will automatically identify phases, tasks, and priority levels, accelerating project creation by replacing manual entry with one-click scheduling.
Analyze intent through multi-turn chats
Supports continuous contextual chat and web searches, as well as end-to-end operations such as generating, modifying, and switching mind maps/slides within the same chat session, allowing you to get everything done without switching windows.
Break down YouTube videos with one click
Paste a YouTube link and the AI assistant will automatically generate a mind map, structured summary, or transcript with timestamps — no need to watch the video, just paste and go. A one-hour video can be condensed into a single image in just 30s.
Shapes & Text Boxes Upgrades
1. Added layer order settings: customize the stacking order of shapes and text boxes for better content organization.
2. Added multi-select to select shapes and text boxes in bulk, improving batch operation efficiency.
3. Added grouping: combine multiple shapes and text boxes for unified moving and editing.
4. Added shortcuts for movement, allowing precise position adjustments for shapes, text boxes, and smart connectors via keyboard.
Path: Editor top menu → Shapes, Text
One-Click Mind Map Generation Enhancements
1. Automatically generate summaries, tags, images, and tables, with support for multiple layouts such as Fishbone Diagram, Timeline, Bracket Diagram, Matrix, and Tree Table to fit different structured presentation needs.
2. Path: Home right panel → Text to Mind Map
